meta.title: cred

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF. Parts, glosses, context sentences and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: cred is the closing plant: a family built entirely around one axis of doubt and trust, quietly setting up the Orchard's future doubt cluster (Bible §5.2 names credulous/incredulous directly) without any code coupling to it.

root.label: cred
root.mentor_note: cred is the one root behind an entire family of doubt and trust.

attempt.direction_prompt: Is he doubting, or believing too easily?

context_sentences: The credulous investor accepted every promise the scheme's founder made, without once demanding evidence.
Even the usually credulous crowd grew suspicious once the second envelope arrived empty.
The witness gave a credible account, consistent in every detail with the earlier report.
Without a credible source, the newspaper refused to run the story at all.
The rescue seemed incredible until the footage confirmed every detail of it.
She described an incredible coincidence that even her closest friends struggled to accept.
The professor stayed incredulous even after being shown the original document twice.
His incredulous stare told the class exactly what he thought of the excuse.

construct_options.text: Unable or unwilling to believe something
Impossible to believe under any circumstance
Certain that something is completely true

context_sentences: No one questioned her credential once the certificate was produced.
The committee asked every applicant to present a credential proving the claimed experience.

construct_options.text: Proof of someone's ability or trustworthiness
A firm belief that cannot be shaken
A formal accusation that must be answered
